summary: Install-time git clone from a personal fork on a mutable branch overwrites the shipped package code
details: setup.py performs an install-time git clone of https://github.com/gabry-lab/index-tts at branch 'dev-3.12' (no commit SHA, no tag, no hash verification) during egg_info / build_py / bdist_wheel and copies the cloned tree over src/indextts, which becomes the code installed and executed by the `indextts` CLI. The clone target is a personal-account fork while the README advertises the upstream project as github.com/index-tts/index-tts — the package's PyPI metadata and documentation point at one publisher while install-time fetches code from another. The branch is mutable: anyone with push access to gabry-lab/index-tts dev-3.12 can change the code that gets executed on installer machines without publishing a new PyPI version, and there is no commit pin or signature check to detect substitution. This pattern (install-time fetch of unpinned code from a non-publisher repository, copied directly into the package's executable code path) is the install-time-rce shape: arbitrary code controlled by a third party runs as a consequence of `pip install indextts-cli`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
